description_long: This object consists of three metal open-ended wrenches, each showing significant signs of age and use, such as rust and surface discoloration. The wrenches are arranged upright, leaning against each other, forming a tripod-like structure. Each wrench features a classic double-ended design with U-shaped jaws of different sizes at either end, suitable for gripping and turning nuts or bolts. The surfaces are rough and matte, with visible pitting and corrosion, especially along the handles and jaws. The handles are straight and lack any ergonomic grip pattern, indicating a utilitarian, industrial design. The overall coloration is a mix of brownish rust and darkened steel, suggesting prolonged exposure to moisture and wear.
description: A group of three old, rusted open-ended metal wrenches with visible wear and corrosion, arranged upright and leaning together. The wrenches have a utilitarian, industrial look with no grip pattern and a rough, matte finish.
description_view_0: Three rusted wrenches are seen upright, with the open jaws clearly visible and the handles showing heavy corrosion and wear.
description_view_1: The wrenches are viewed from a slight angle, highlighting the thickness of the handles and the uneven rust patterns along the sides.
description_view_2: This view shows the wrenches from the opposite side, emphasizing the different jaw sizes and the extent of surface rust on the handles.
description_view_3: The wrenches are seen from a rear angle, with the handles and jaws overlapping, making the corrosion and aged metal texture prominent.
attribute_rust_or_wear_marks: Extensive rust and wear marks are visible across all surfaces, especially on the handles and jaws, with heavy pitting and discoloration.
